children.text: in a manner that conserves, protects, and enhances the resources of the Conservation Area; and

children.label: A
children.text: the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.); and43 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: B
children.text: other applicable law, including this Act.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary, in consultation with the State, the city of Henderson, the County, and any other interested persons, shall develop a management plan for the Conservation Area.November 6, 2002

children.label: A
children.text: describe the appropriate uses and management of the Conservation Area;

children.label: I
children.text: for installing, repairing, maintaining, and reconstructing water development projects, including guzzlers, that would enhance the Conservation Area by promoting healthy, viable, and more naturally distributed wildlife populations; and

children.label: II
children.text: subject to any limitations that are not more restrictive than the limitations on such uses authorized in wilderness areas under section 208; and1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: include or provide recommendations on ways of minimizing the visual impacts of such activities on the Conservation Area;

children.label: C
children.text: include a plan for litter cleanup and public lands awareness campaign on public lands in and around the Conservation Area; and

children.label: D
children.text: include a recommendation on the location for a right-of-way for a rural roadway to provide the city of Henderson with access to the Conservation Area, in accordance with the application numbered N–65874.

children.heading: Uses
children.label: c Uses
children.text: The Secretary shall allow only such uses of the Conservation Area that the Secretary determines will further the purpose described in .section 460qqq of this title

children.heading: Motorized vehicles
children.label: d Motorized vehicles
children.text: Except as needed for administrative purposes or to respond to an emergency, the use of motorized vehicles in the Conservation Area shall be permitted only on roads and trails designated for the use of motorized vehicles by the management plan developed under subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: all forms of entry and appropriation under the public land laws;

children.label: B
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: C
children.text: operation of the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws.

children.heading: Additional land
children.label: 2 Additional land
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, if the Secretary acquires mineral or other interests in a parcel of land within the Conservation Area after , the parcel is withdrawn from operation of the laws referred to in paragraph (1) on the date of acquisition of the land.November 6, 2002

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter affects the jurisdiction of the State with respect to fish and wildlife, including hunting, fishing, and trapping in the Conservation Area.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: A Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may designate by regulation areas in which, and establish periods during which, for reasons of public safety, administration, or compliance with applicable laws, no hunting, fishing, or trapping will be permitted in the Conservation Area.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: B Consultation
children.text: Except in emergencies, the Secretary shall consult with the appropriate State agency before promulgating regulations under subparagraph (A) that close a portion of the Conservation Area to hunting, fishing, or trapping.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The establishment of the Conservation Area shall not create an express or implied protective perimeter or buffer zone around the Conservation Area.

children.heading: Private land
children.label: 2 Private land
children.text: If the use of, or conduct of an activity on, private land that shares a boundary with the Conservation Area is consistent with applicable law, nothing in this subchapter concerning the establishment of the Conservation Area shall prohibit or limit the use or conduct of the activity.

children.label: A
children.text: performing geotechnical investigations within the rights-of-way; and

children.label: B
children.text: constructing and operating water transmission and related facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Authority may, without consideration, excavate and use or dispose of sand, gravel, minerals, or other materials from the tunneling of the water pipeline necessary to fulfill the purpose of the rights-of-way granted under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Memorandum of understanding
children.label: B Memorandum of understanding
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the rights-of-way are granted under paragraph (1), the Secretary and the Authority shall enter into a memorandum of understanding identifying Federal land on which the Authority may dispose of materials under subparagraph (A) to further the interests of the Bureau of Land Management.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary may include reasonable terms and conditions, consistent with section 505 of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 (), as are necessary to protect Conservation Area resources.43 U.S.C. 1765

children.label: B
children.text: Construction of the water pipeline shall not permanently adversely affect conservation area surface resources.

children.label: C
children.text: The right-of-way shall not be located through or under any area designated as wilderness.
